chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next, I have this piece of code.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have to analyze and find the time complexity of this one.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this code also for loop is used.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But loop is starting from 1 going up to n, that is less than n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And it is being multiplied by 2, i.e. multiplied by 2 every time.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So blindly we cannot say this will execute for n times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We have to analyze this one.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us see how many times this will execute.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is not n, we don't know how many times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I will take the i value.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Initially it is 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The next time it is going to be 1 into 2, that is 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The next time it will be 2 into 2 again, so it will be 2 square.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See every time it is getting multiplied by 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then it is 2 square into 2 again, so this will be 2 power 3, goes on.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How many times?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We don't know.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us say this will execute for 2 power k times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we know that this will terminate when i becomes greater than equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we assume that when it has reached for some k number of times it has repeated, assume

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: i became greater than equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then what is our i value?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 2 power k.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Since i is equal to 2 power k, therefore 2 power k has become greater than equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now if I take 2 power k is equal to n, then k is how much?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Log n base 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, this is the kind of complexity.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the statement will execute for order of log n base 2 times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So from this we can observe that if you have a loop where the counter variable is not incrementing

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: but is getting multiplied by something, let us say 2 or 3, then it will get log base 2

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it will execute for log base 2 times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now I have an incremental for loop, the regular loop that we use.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us analyze that one and then we will analyze once again here the other method I will show you.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See here i is initially 1 and it is incrementing by 1, plus 1, then plus 1, then plus 1, goes

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: on, plus 1 and it will stop and finally it becomes greater than n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So up to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So how many times it is getting added?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us say k times and that is equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So how many times?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: k times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What is that k?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us check this one.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: i is initially 1, then it is next time into 2, then again into 2, then again into 2, so

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: up to some times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So finally it will stop and it becomes greater than or equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then this is how many times?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will be let us say k times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what will be the result?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This will be 2 power k.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That will be equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So k is how much?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Log n base 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the other way we have analyzed this one.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that is incrementing 1, 1, 1, so k times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is multiplying by 2, 2, 2 every time.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So k times it is getting multiplied.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is 2 power k that is equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So k is how many times?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: k is log n base 2 times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if you have any loop like this, the value of i is getting multiplied, then it is going

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: to take log n times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So you can take it as a formula now.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The time complex it taken by this algorithm as log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The statement will be for log n times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now when you take log n, log n may give decimal values, float values.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we need to know whether I have to take float or seal of that value.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that is important.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Means shall we truncate it or round up the value.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for that let us take some example and check it.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose n value is 8.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now i value, let us trace this one by taking a sample value.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: i is 1 initially, then i is multiplied by 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is 2, 2 is less than 8, 2 is less than 8.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then again multiplied by 2, 4.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 4 is less than 8.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n is 8, right?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So again multiplied by 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now 8 is not less than 8, so it will stop.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So total how many times it is repeating?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is repeating for 3 times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The next if I take the n value as 10.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I write the n value as 10, let us see what happens.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: i is initially 1, 1 is less than 10.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now n value is 10, 1 is less than 10.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Continue.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is multiplied by 2, so 2 is less than 10.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 4, 4 is less than 10.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 8 multiplied by 2, it is 8.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 8 is also less than 10.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then 16, so 16 is i, i is not less than 10.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it will stop here.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So total how many times? 4 times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is executing for 4 times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I find log 8, log 8 will be 3.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Log 8 is written as 2.3 base 2

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and it is written as 3 log 2 base 2

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and this is 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So answer is 3.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now same way if I find out log 10 base 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now 10 is not an exact powers of 2,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: so I may not get the decimal value, integer value.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I may get a decimal value.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Right?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us take it as example 3.2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I am assuming it as 3.2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I got 3.2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But if you see the number of times repeated, 4 times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it should be taken as seal value.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this log should be taken as seal.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: When you use log sometimes whether it is seal or floor,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: that is also important.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Other piece of code here, let us see how it is going to execute.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What will be the time complexity of this one?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See here, i is starting from n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next time i value is n by 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The next time it is n by 2 square.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then n by 2 cube.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So on.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How many times?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n by 2 power k.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We do not know the number of times, so we are assuming it as 4k times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And we know that the loop will terminate when i becomes less than 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it will continue as long as it is greater than or equal to 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So assume i has become less than 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So therefore, what is i value?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 2 power k is less than 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if I equate it, then it is going to be 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And n is equal to 2 power k.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So k is how much?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Log n base 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So again, the time for this one is order of log n base 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the time complexity of this piece of code is also log n base 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the previous one, in the 5th example, I have shown you that it was multiplying every time and starting from 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now this is dividing every time and starting from n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And up to what?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Up to 1 it is reaching.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is order of log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next piece of code is the 7th example I am taking here.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So here, i is starting from 0 and i into i is less than or equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So how many times this will execute?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This will execute as long as i into i is less than n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But it will terminate when i into i is greater than or equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We assume that i into i square is equal to n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what is i? It is root n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I don't have to k here. I have not used k here.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have directly used i only.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: i into i is less than n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is a square. If you send that side, it is a root.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it will execute for root n times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now the next example.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here I have two for loops.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: These are independent loops, one after another.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Don't mistake it as nested loops.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: They are not one inside another.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the time complexity for this one is

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this is an independent loop and it is iterating for n times

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and it is an incremental loop.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this is also an incremental loop.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is also order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if you combine them and take their time,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: that is n plus n, that is 2n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And it is order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is an independent loop and the time is order of n only.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n plus n, that is order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is not n square.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now the next one.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have two loops here, independent loops.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This next loop, I have to find out what is the time taken by this statement.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I observe only this one,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then j is taking values from 1,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: j is less than n,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: j is incrementing, that is updating by multiplying by 2 every time.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is going to take log value.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Log of what? Log of p.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is log p.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What is p?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you see the previous loop, here p is incremented.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So p is incremented in this loop.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this p will take the value that number of times this loop is repeating.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How many times this loop is repeating?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I take the values 1, 1 to n,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and I assign i into 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So again it is log.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Log of what? It is log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So p value will be log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this statement will repeat for log p times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what is p?

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: p is evaluated here, and it is used here.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is log of log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As p is equal to log n, so it is log of log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the time complexity is order of log of log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next example, here I have nested for loop.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The outer loop is taking i and assign 0 i is less than n,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and i is incrementing.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is an increment loop, so this is order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And whatever is there inside will repeat for n times.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next, the inner for loop,

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this is using j.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: j is less than n, and j is every time multiplied by 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will take log n time.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this itself will take log n time.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And anything inside that also will take log n time.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you remember, initially I have shown you that this takes n plus 1.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: No, it will not be any useful if you write n plus 1 or don't write it.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It makes no difference.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Just you can take it as n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now total how much? 2n log n plus n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So highest term is this one.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is order of n log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the time complexity of this one is order of n log n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us write some pieces of code and see what the time complexities that we have got so far.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See if I write a for loop for i assign 0, i is less than n, i plus plus.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For this we got the time as order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is an incrementing loop.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If this is written as i assign 0, i is less than n, i assign i plus 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is going to be n by 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That also will take order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then for i assign n, i is greater than 1, i minus minus.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This also order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if it is incrementing also order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If it is decrementing also order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then for i assign 1, i less than n, i assign i multiplied by 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is order of log n base 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it means for i assign 1, i is less than n, i assign i into 3.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This will be order of log n base 3.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For i assign n, i is greater than 1 and i assign i by 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is order of log n base 2.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So now we can take these as formulas.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If a loop is incrementing that is n or decrementing it is n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Whether it is incrementing by 1 or 2 or even 10 or even 100 also it is order of n only we say.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n by 2 is also order of n.

chunks.title: Time Complexity Example #2 - Abdul Bari

text: Next, I have this piece of code. I have to analyze and find the time complexity of this one. And this code also for loop is used. But loop is starting from 1 going up to n, that is less than n. And it is being multiplied by 2, i.e. multiplied by 2 every time. So blindly we cannot say this will execute for n times. We have to analyze this one. Let us see how many times this will execute. It is not n, we don't know how many times. I will take the i value. Initially it is 1. The next time it is going to be 1 into 2, that is 2. The next time it will be 2 into 2 again, so it will be 2 square. See every time it is getting multiplied by 2. Then it is 2 square into 2 again, so this will be 2 power 3, goes on. How many times? We don't know. So let us say this will execute for 2 power k times. Now we know that this will terminate when i becomes greater than equal to n. So we assume that when it has reached for some k number of times it has repeated, assume i became greater than equal to n. Then what is our i value? 2 power k. Since i is equal to 2 power k, therefore 2 power k has become greater than equal to n. Now if I take 2 power k is equal to n, then k is how much? Log n base 2. Yes, this is the kind of complexity. So the statement will execute for order of log n base 2 times. So from this we can observe that if you have a loop where the counter variable is not incrementing but is getting multiplied by something, let us say 2 or 3, then it will get log base 2 times. So it will execute for log base 2 times. Now I have an incremental for loop, the regular loop that we use. Continue. It is multiplied by 2, so 2 is less than 10. 4, 4 is less than 10. 8 multiplied by 2, it is 8. 8 is also less than 10. Then 16, so 16 is i, i is not less than 10. So it will stop here. So total how many times? 4 times. It is executing for 4 times. If I find log 8, log 8 will be 3. How? Log 8 is written as 2.3 base 2 and it is written as 3 log 2 base 2 and this is 1. So answer is 3. So if I equate it, then it is going to be 1. And n is equal to 2 power k. So k is how much? Log n base 2. So again, the time for this one is order of log n base 2. This also order of n. So if it is incrementing also order of n. If it is decrementing also order of n. Then for i assign 1, i less than n, i assign i multiplied by 2. This is order of log n base 2. So it means for i assign 1, i is less than n, i assign i into 3. This will be order of log n base 3. For i assign n, i is greater than 1 and i assign i by 2. It is order of log n base 2. So now we can take these as formulas. If a loop is incrementing that is n or decrementing it is n.
